title: Hantavirus: Many unknowns surround an 'unprecedented and worrying' outbreak
subhed: The WHO has sought to reassure the public about the risk of a pandemic. A strain which is transmissible between humans via saliva droplets was identified in three patients on Wednesday, on a cruise ship bound for the Canary Islands.

Rubio steps up as defender of Trump's erratic Iran policy
subhed: Long seen as a Republican hawk during his tenure in Congress as Florida's senator, Marco Rubio has achieved a delicate balance: aligning himself with the president's positions while suggesting his own distinctiveness.

title: Gaza police become targets for Israeli army
subhed: Police forces under Hamas's authority are regularly targeted by the Israeli army. Israel describes these as preventive strikes, which target officers who also serve in the Islamist movement's armed wing. Civil society actors, on the other hand, accuse Israel of seeking to plunge the territory into chaos.

title: Unstoppable PSG withstands Bayern to reach second consecutive Champions League final
subhed: Eight days after a spectacular victory at the Parc des Princes, Paris Saint-Germain secured qualification in a return leg that felt like a battle on German soil.

title: Welsh nationalists poised for historic election breakthrough
subhed: For the first time in its history, the pro-independence Welsh party Plaid Cymru could top the Welsh Parliament in Thursday's election. The surge reflects not just disillusionment with rivals but a deeper frustration at years of being sidelined by London.

Scottish elections: Reform UK capitalizes on anti-migrant protests in Falkirk
subhed: As regional elections take place on Thursday, May 7, in Scotland and Wales, far-right activists have managed to rally broad support around slogans hostile to asylum seekers in a region previously known for being welcoming.

title: Jean Pisani-Ferry: 'The United States has lost its moral authority for good'
subhed: The United States is turning its back on democracy and freedom – values the nation once embodied – and is now replacing them with the law of the strongest and a culture of deal-making. Donald Trump has caused irreparable harm to the world's leading power, argues the economist in his column.

title: French architects of Iran nuclear deal watch Trump unravel their work
subhed: French diplomats who helped negotiate the 2015 Vienna agreement, aimed to prevent Tehran from acquiring nuclear weapons, now watch bitterly as decisions by the Trump administration unravel their work.
